title: AquaVision Knowledge Repository — Schema Registry
description: Defines the shape of every recurring object in this repository so that a consuming webpage can render any file generically. Covers the whole AquaVision platform across its three phases (free app, premium tier, Phase 3 OmniDrone/IoT hardware), not a single-product schema. These are JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) fragments plus prose notes.

conventions.identifiers: kebab-case, globally unique within the repository, permanent. Labels may be edited freely; ids may not.
conventions.units: SI unless the field name states otherwise. Currency fields always carry an explicit ISO-4217 `currency` sibling. Never store a bare number for money.
conventions.dates: ISO 8601. Full dates as YYYY-MM-DD. Years as integers. Ranges as {start, end}.
conventions.nulls: Use null for 'known to be absent'. Use the string 'unknown' for 'we have not determined this'. Never use 0 as a stand-in for missing.
conventions.citations: Any factual claim carries `citation_ids: []` referencing entries in data/07_research/references-bibliography.json.
conventions.provenance: Mandatory on every quantitative record. See enum below.

provenance.description: The epistemic status of the numbers in this record. Consuming UIs SHOULD render a visible badge for anything that is not `verified_external`, and MUST render a warning badge for `illustrative_synthetic`.

confidence.description: How much weight the authors place on this record surviving contact with reality.

authors.description: Family, Given format. Use the issuing body for institutional authors.

accessed.description: ISO date the URL was last checked.

apa7.description: Pre-rendered APA 7th edition string for copy-paste into a manuscript.

relevance.description: One or two sentences on why this source matters to the AquaVision platform argument (whichever phase it supports — free app, premium tier, or the Phase 3 OmniDrone/IoT layer). This is the field a thesis writer actually needs.

verification_status.description: cited_from_search_result means the bibliographic details came from a search engine summary and the authors have NOT opened the source. Verify before submitting a thesis.

description.description: What the reader should take away. Rendered as a caption.

provenance.description: Overrides the chart-level provenance for this series. Use when a chart mixes historical fact with projection.

data.description: Array of [x, y] pairs or {x, y, meta} objects.

annotations.description: Threshold lines, event markers, shaded regions.

definition.description: The precise operational definition. If two people can compute different numbers from this, the definition is not finished.

statement.description: Shall-statement. One requirement per record, testable, unambiguous.

trace_to.description: IDs of the problem, use case, or stakeholder need this requirement serves. Every requirement must trace to something or it is scope creep.

detectability.description: FMEA detection. 1 = certain to be caught, 10 = undetectable before harm.

rpn.description: Risk Priority Number = severity x likelihood x detectability. Recompute on every edit.

model_card.description: Follows the Mitchell et al. (2019) Model Cards convention. See citation_id 'mitchell-model-cards-2019'.

out_of_scope_use.description: Explicitly what this model must NOT be used to decide. Required — a model card without this is marketing.

performance.description: null until real evaluation has been run. Do not populate with aspirational numbers; use `target_performance` for those.

human_oversight.description: Where a human must remain in the loop and why.

study_record.description: One entry in a thematic related-studies collection.

summary.description: What the study did and found.

relevance_to_omnidrone.description: The 'so what'. Which AquaVision platform design decision or claim does this support or challenge — including but not limited to Phase 3 OmniDrone hardware decisions? Field name kept as-is for id/reference stability across the repository; scope is the whole platform, not just the drone.

supports_or_challenges.description: Intellectual honesty field. A literature review that only contains supporting studies is not a literature review.

basis_year.description: Real terms base year, for inflation adjustment.

validation_notes: This registry is descriptive, not enforced by a build step. If you add a file, add its shape here.
A consuming webpage should treat any record missing `provenance` as untrusted and render it greyed out.
The `illustrative_synthetic` badge is a load-bearing safety feature of this repository, not decoration. Do not strip it in the UI.
